llcFilesWith: Rhode Island Department of State, Business Services Division (Fictitious Business Name Statement, Form 624B, under § 7-16-9), filed through the online Corporate Filing System
soleProprietorFilesWith: Rhode Island Department of State (trade name registration; Rhode Island recently moved trade names from its 39 city and town clerks to the Department of State, though a trade name is still limited to one municipality)
feeBasis: An LLC's Fictitious Business Name Statement costs $50 (a $2.50 enhanced-access fee applies online). A sole proprietor's trade name registration costs $20.
renewalRule: A fictitious business name statement stays on file until the LLC amends or cancels it; there is no periodic renewal.
protectionNote: A fictitious business name or trade name discloses the name a business uses and grants no exclusive right; a sole proprietor's trade name is limited to a single city or town rather than the whole state.
searchNote: Fictitious business names and trade names registered with the Department of State appear in the online business search.
notes: Rhode Island splits its DBA vocabulary by filer but now files both at the state level. An LLC that trades under another name files a Fictitious Business Name Statement with the Department of State ($50, § 7-16-9). Individuals and general partnerships register a trade name ($20); Rhode Island recently centralized trade names from its 39 municipalities to the Department of State, but a trade name is still tied to one city or town rather than being statewide. Neither filing grants an exclusive right and neither requires publication.

sources.title: The Rhode Island Limited Liability Company Act — Name; Fictitious business names (LLC $50)
Filing of Trade Name — business trade name requirements (sole proprietors)
